solution.rawText:
Let us label the midpoint of OR as T , so that we can refer to it.
We first observe that since all the edges of the pyramid are of length 20m, the triangles are all
equilateral.
There are two ways to get from P to the midpoint of OR in a short distance: one can either
travel along the base to RS and then along the triangular face to T , or one could travel along
the triangular face OP S to the edge OS and from there to T . It is not obvious which route is
shorter, so we will calculate both. (There are also routes which are mirror-reflections of these
going via the edge QR or OQ.)
To find these distances, we can вҖңunfoldвҖқ the tetrahedron, that is, make a net for the tetrahedron.
For the first route, we draw just the faces P QRS and ORS:

                                                   O

                                                        T

                                        S                   R




                                            P            Q

                                                       вҲҡ       вҲҡ            вҲҡ
The height of the triangle OSR is 20 sin 60в—Ұ = 20 Г— 23 = 10 3, so T lies 5 3 above the line SR;
T also lies 34 Г— 20 = 15 to the right of S (as it is at the midpoint of OR). We can therefore find
the length P T using PythagorasвҖҷs theorem:
                                                         вҲҡ
                                  P T 2 = 152 + (20 + 5 3)2
                                                            вҲҡ
                                        = 225 + 400 + 200 3 + 75
                                                     вҲҡ
                                        = 700 + 200 3

We now look at the other route, which we can do by drawing the triangles OP S and OSR:

                                                    O
                                                        T
                                    P                            R


                                                    S

                                                                   вҲҡ
Using

5 3 to its right, while T lies 5 above P (being a quarter of OS). PythagorasвҖҷs theorem therefore
gives
                                                  вҲҡ
                                       P T 2 = (15 3)2 + 52
                                                = 225 Г— 3 + 25
                                                = 700

This is therefore the shorter of the two routes, and its length is       700 = 10 7, hence the solution
is option D.
